markers.title: Eternal Blaze Ironworks
markers.description:
Willowshore's blacksmith, the kitsune Yong Wu-Xiu. She has mastered many metal working skills and can make high quality arms and armor, though locally she's most known for her exceptional quality cookware.

Services: crafting metal items
Wares: metalworks, adventuring gear, cookware, metal armor, metal weapons, metal shields

markers.title: Willowshore Stables
markers.description:
Willowshore Stables is the town’s veterinary facility for household animals, pets, and livestock. Incidentally, Willowshore Stables’ use of livestock as beasts of burden influenced the village’s food culture, causing many households to avoid the consumption of beef. The owner, Kum Soon-chong, and his teenage son, Meng-sung, are well-known for their affable attitudes and compassion for all kinds of animals.

Services: medical treatment for animals
Wares: animal rentals, barding, saddle bags, carts

markers.title: Abadar Shrine
markers.description: This elegant shrine houses a 3-foot-tall limestone statue of Abadar that holds a shovel in one hand and a trowel in the other. The statue was reportedly taken to Goka first to ritualistically invite Abadar’s divine blessings at the Grand Bank before being ferried back to Willowshore.

markers.title: Graveside Manners
markers.description:
The tombs in this old cemetery house the urns of many of those early settlers. Today, only a few families use Graveside Manners as a burial ground. The headstones remain free of lichen and moss thanks to the graveyard’s keeper, You So-Jin who maintains an herbalist shop—the primary reason anyone visits Graveside Manners today.

Services: alchemy lab for rent
Wares: alchemical elixirs and tools, alchemical reagents, fresh and dried herbs

markers.title: Silvermist Lodge
markers.description:
The Silvermist Lodges are split between three small compounds in the woods south of town. Aiomena now runs the hunting lodges from the central compound.

Wares: animal parts, snare supplies

markers.title: Matsuki Estate
markers.description: “Old” Matsuki once apprenticed to the head carpenter of the Tan Sugi monastery. After taking leave from his master, he led his family to construct a large estate to house all two dozen of them. Reportedly, the superstitious patriarch then made sure that the outer walls were whitewashed with limestone containing blessed salts and holy water.

markers.title: Thrice Blessed Inn
markers.description:
Originally the Thrice Blessed Brothel, this business now operates as an inn under its half-elven owner Mother Otis. Its heavy shutters (for privacy) and the rumoured blessing of three different gods (Kofusachi, Calistria and Shelyn) are all that remains of its original purpose.

Services: lodging
Wares: food and drink

markers.title: Meilin's House
markers.description: Meilin's cabin by the edge of the woods.

markers.title: Kaseru's House
markers.description: Kaseru's house, near the riverbank by the Milling Houses.

markers.title: Milling Houses
markers.description:
Work at the mills is intense, and the hours can be irregular due to the demand each day. The millers’ leader, Choe Chung-hu, keeps morale high with encouragement, pushing back against unreasonable demands, keeping wages stable, and offering midnight snacks.

Wares: de-husked grain, grain flower and stone objects

markers.title: Nine Ear Shrine
markers.description:
This shrine to Daikitsu looks like an unassuming mud-wall hut from the outside. Inside it descends 10 feet underground into an enclosed space lined with stone, in the middle of which sits a 15-foot-tall marble statue of a nine-tailed fox. A large spiritual rope made of hay hangs off its neck. The walls of the basement are decorated with painted carvings that depict stories of Daikitsu.

Services: fulu crafting, scribe scroll (divine or primal), limited talisman crafting
Wares: fulus, scrolls, talismans

markers.title: Lady of Souls
markers.description:
A cathedral to Pharasma, run by the second-generation Taldan woman Elizath Candora, who believes she is here as fate arranged by the goddess herself. She’s fond of spending time alone in the graveyard with her flute and often plays the instrument there to help soothe the spirits of those recently buried therein.

Services: scribe scroll (divine), limited (non)magical healing
Wares: scrolls, consumable religious items

markers.title: Dawnstep Bridge
markers.description: A stone bridge some seven feet above the water, connecting Southshore and Northridge, as well as the location of the Eternal Lantern. Shenmen custom dictates the latern must remain lit at all times lest the town appear abandoned, which would invite vermin, ghosts, dangerous beasts and worse into the town.

markers.title: Industrial District
markers.description:
The industrial heart of Willowshore, which includes several noticable businesses:

Jadeite Essentials, a distillery and glasblowing facility, managed by the half-elf Shioli.

Wares: alcohol, essential oils and perfumes, glassware and glass items

Cloud Paper House, a paper mill run by tengu Kawaka who is also leader of the labourer's guild.
Wares: paper and paper products such as spellbooks and formula books

Rebel's Leatherworks, a tannery run by the grumpy nagaji Vitumniar.
Wares: hide and leather items

markers.title: Woodraft Lake
markers.description: When the construction of the Willowshore Dam created this lake it is said to have submerged a shrine to Lady Nanbyo, and the lake is still treated as a shrine to the Widow of Suffering.

markers.title: Willowshore Dam
markers.description: Willowshore’s dam is a minor engineering marvel. Its main body is constructed out of immense, precisely fitted slabs of dark-gray marble, carved with a carefully calculated curvature. The dam has two giant wooden gates that can be closed or opened to lower the level of the lake. Turning the wheel that controls the gates automatically causes a bell to ring, signaling anyone downriver to move away to safety.

markers.title: Downtown Willowshore
markers.description: This area is a major hub of activity in town. A detail of Downtown Willowshore is dispayed on the top left of the map.

markers.title: Imperial Baracks
markers.description:
The Imperial Baracks house the Willowshore guard. Once commanded by Granny Hu, now the stoic Zheng Peng leads the guard.
Wares: armor, gear maintenance equipment, weapons

markers.title: Flint's Flat
markers.description: A high-priced room downtown that Flint rents.

markers.title: Treesparrow's Rest
markers.description:
One of Willowshore's busiest stores, the Treesparrow's Nest is a family-owned grocery store run by Nadoya Sanmi.
Wares: alcohol, grains, preservd foods, rations

markers.title: The Happy Kappa
markers.description:
The Happy Kappa is Willowshore's public bathhouse, run by the chatty halfling Hua De-ge. It is known as a place of relaxation, but also a good place to find some gossip.

Services: relaxing soaks, gossip

markers.title: Seven Coloured Songbird Theatre
markers.description: The outdoor stage is frequently used to show Shu Opera performances, orchestrated by the gregarious kitsune director Kazuma Oono, who owns the theatre.

markers.title: Governor's Mansion
markers.description: The governor’s home and office, which also serves as the village’s bank and treasury. It is a stately, large building befitting an imperial governor, even if that empire has collapsed.

markers.title: Luo and Laws
markers.description:
The office of Luo Xi Yang, an eager female lawyer who is an expert at the complex laws that still govern Willowshore, in part because of her very extensive legal and historical library.

Services: eclectic and historical library

markers.title: Woodcarver's Guild
markers.description:
To ensure the lumber lords would never favor anyone, the woodcarvers came together to form a guild, through which young apprentices have the opportunity to intern at different shops during their apprenticeship. Yun Mong-Un, the woodcarver’s current elected guild leader, is a product of such training. Old Matsuki is a highly respected figure here as well, having trained many of the current master crafters.

Services: non-weapon wooden item crafting, artwork and jewerly crafting
Wares: artwork, wood items, wooden religious symbols

markers.title: Second Best
markers.description:
Run by the inventor Huo Tian-Zhe, Second Best is a shop where all sorts of gadgets, tools and devices can be found. Huo Tian-Zhe occasionally collaborates with Yung Wu-Xiu of the Eternal Blaze on maintaining the town's dam.

Services: craft alchemical ammunition and boms, craft metal items
Wares: adventuring tools, alchemical ammunition, alchemical bombs, clockwork gadgets, simple armor, metal weapons and shields

markers.title: The Hand Of Spring
markers.description:
The practice of doctor Damihansig Mababangloob, whom everyone calls Dami for short. Dami uses a mix of acupuncture, purified chemicals, and surgery to provide care. Though his treatments aren’t what Willowshore is used to, Dami has developed quite a reputation for being a charitable doctor who cares more about his patients than monetary gain.

Services: basic non-magical healing
Wares: alchemical healing items, healer's kit supplies, non-magical healing items

markers.title: Mother's Coil
markers.description:
A tower of dark basalt in the Nagajor style created overnight some 15 years ago by the late nagaji wizard Anjal of Thulsadus. This little corner of arcane study is now managed by Anjal's former apprentice, Igawa Jubei.

Services: scribe scroll, craft wand
Wares: arcane and occult scrolls, formulas and recipes for magic items and spells, wands

markers.title: Ceiba-Duyue Exchange
markers.description:
The heart of Willowshore's trade with the outside world. While the Kofusachi priest Kim Gu-won officially manages the exchange, this is also the residence of “Granny” Hu Ban-niang, the cunning matriarch that leads the Northridge faction. The exchange is also home to a shrine to Kofusachi, Tian god of joy and prosperity.

Services: caligraphy, scribe scroll, fulu crafting
Wares: beverages, divine scrolls, foods and spices, fulus, raw minerals, textiles

markers.title: Fisheries
markers.description:
Freshwater fish is an important food source for Willowshore. Locals traditionally capture entire schools of fish, preserve those above a certain size, and raise small ones in their backyard ponds. Zataku, a female ratfolk fisher, is Willowshore’s foremost fish keeper; her ability to sense weather changes gives her the perfect edge in her occupation.

Services: weather forecasting
Wares: dried or smoked fish and shrimp, fertilizer, fishing gear, insect bait

markers.title: Dock
markers.description:
After the construction of the dam, Willowshore’s old dock was blocked off, thus requiring the construction of a new dock at the mouth of the Ceiba River. The new dock is operated and managed by Rajul Samudra, who was hired by the Ceiba-Duyue Exchange specifically for his boat-building skills.

Services: boat rental and sales

markers.title: Mushroom Warehouse
markers.description:
Everyone knows that mushrooms aren’t the only things growing in the farmhouse, though. For the right price, the Mushroom House can grow almost anything. The smuggler Luda Bama, a spry, elderly halfling woman, is the head of this illicit market, which she runs with the explicit permission of Granny Hu.

Wares: adventuring gear, drugs, poisons, tattoos

markers.title: Cerulean Teahouse
markers.description: A fancy teahouse constructed for the lumber lords, it has been vacant since the collapse of Lung Wa and the departure of the lumber lords. It's now slowly falling into ruin, with only Yami, a local semi-feral house cat, calling it home.

markers.title: Abandoned Estates
markers.description: The abandoned lumber lords’ estates have been thoroughly picked clean. One of the houses features prominent butterfly and star motifs.

markers.title: The Leshy's Salon
markers.description:
Leshies have lived in the Willowshore area long before the town was founded, but eventually joined the town and set up their own tea farm business. The Salon sells tea, tea ware and pottery, managed by the leshy Mountain Summit Grass.

Wares: clay pottery tea ware, tea leaves

markers.title: Bones of the Forgotten
markers.description: An unkept and overgrown area where the bodies of serious criminals are buried. While graves were unmarked in the past, a few wooden posts have been erected recently and paper shuriken hung off them, either a silent protest that the ones buried beneath might have died an unjust death or an attempt to stave off the rise of a bitter, vengeful ghost

markers.title: The Great Willow
markers.description: An ancient willow tree of unusual size at the summit of the lowest of these mountains. Protected by the kodama kami “Great Willow” who knows much about the woods of Willowshore and their history.

markers.title: Spider Gate
markers.description: Named for the large stone spider statue that the locals believe houses a guardian spirit that protects Willowshore. Named Ugly Cute it holds a lantern in its fangs. While not as prestigious as the Eternal Lantern on Dawnstep bridge, the villagers strive to keep it lit at all times.

markers.title: Silvermist Lodge
markers.description:
The Silvermist Lodges are split between three small compounds in the woods south of town. Aiomena now runs the hunting lodges from the central compound.

Wares: animal parts, snare supplies

markers.title: Silvermist Lodge
markers.description:
The Silvermist Lodges are split between three small compounds in the woods south of town. Aiomena now runs the hunting lodges from the central compound.

Wares: animal parts, snare supplies

markers.title: Matsu's Home
markers.description: Matsu is a gregarious, retired chef. He is a lifelong bachelor who enjoys participating in village life.

markers.title: Mama Bao's
markers.description:
Nakamura Yu Ming, also known affectionately as Mama Bao runs this small steamed buns shop - a favourite for many locals. The selection is limited but the quality is exceptional.

Wares: steamed buns

markers.title: Ho Jeong-Hui's Home
markers.description: Home of the handsome guard Ho Jeong-Hui, Here, he gets to spend time cooking the pungent (but delicious) dinners he loves so much without vexing fellow guards in the barracks. His reputation as one of Willowshore’s most eligible bachelors ensures that few invitations to his home are ignored.

markers.title: Kawaka's Home
markers.description: Most of Willowshore’s citizens live in or at least near to their place of business. The owner of Cloud Paper House, Myna Kawaka is no exception; he lives in a small suite of rooms on an upper floor of the paper mill

markers.title: Eastern Watchtower
markers.description: A watchtower built along the road from Karahai, this is the first building of Willowshore a traveler encounters after passing through the Spider Gate.

markers.title: The Hungry Groundhog
markers.description:
This inn is run by the boisterous dwarf Borgram Strongarm who is originally from the Five Kings Mountains but ended up in Willowshore. The Hungry Groundhog is well known for its eternal stew that Borgram has kept going for the past 20 years at least, and fresh salt water lobster (unusual, given how far from any sea willowshore is).

Services: lodgings
Wares: food and drinks

markers.title: Zhong's Catch
markers.description:
A small stall on the docks where the gnome Zhong specialises in the best fresh fish of the day. The deep fried fish is popular with the dock workers, but Zhong's speciality are his delicate sushi creations.

Wares: fried fish, sushi
